message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker with resize dashboard and proptest progress

Sync .beads/issues.jsonl with latest work-area status reflecting
completed resize dashboard renderer (wa-1u90p.5), CLI integration,
and expanded proptest coverage milestones.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-1brb","title":"[EPIC] FTUI-03 Runtime Ownership and One-Writer Enforcement","description":"## Purpose
Migrate wa terminal runtime ownership to ftui-compatible one-writer discipline.

## Why
Current direct terminal writes, command handoff transitions, and screen-mode changes are high-risk without strict ownership and RAII cleanup guarantees.

## Focus
- single output ownership gate
- inline/alt transitions and command handoff safety
- panic-safe restoration and lifecycle validation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:10.700910498Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:57:16.459782223Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:57:16.459713265Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1brb","depends_on_id":"wa-1k52","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:50.299044205Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-1brb","depends_on_id":"wa-2wed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:10.724993977Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"wa-1bznu","title":"Phase 4: Replace tokio task spawning with asupersync structured concurrency","description":"# Replace tokio task spawning with asupersync structured concurrency

## Goal
Replace all tokio::spawn/JoinHandle/JoinSet with asupersync's structured concurrency (Scope/region). This bead establishes the patterns; per-module application is done in module-specific beads.

## Key paradigm shift
Tokio allows detached tasks (fire-and-forget). Asupersync enforces structured concurrency — every task belongs to a Scope, and the scope waits for all children before exiting.

## Scope (what this bead covers)
- Establish cx.region() / scope.spawn() patterns
- Define how to replace JoinHandle (scope child handle)
- Define how to replace JoinSet (region with multiple spawns + semaphore for bounding)
- Document fire-and-forget redesign strategies:
  - Option A: Attach to long-lived application scope
  - Option B: Dedicated background region for app lifetime
  - Option C: Refactor to not need detached tasks
- Create helper utilities for common patterns (bounded spawn, spawn-with-timeout)

## What this does NOT cover
- select! macro replacement — that's per-module work because each module has different select! branches. Module beads (wa-p48pw, wa-1m7nk, wa-16hou, etc.) handle their own select! migration using asupersync::combinator::select/race.

## Patterns to establish
```rust
// Bounded concurrent work (replaces JoinSet with max)
pub async fn spawn_bounded<F, T>(
    cx: &mut Cx, max: usize, tasks: Vec<F>
) -> Outcome<Vec<T>, Error>
where F: FnOnce(&mut Cx) -> Fut...
{
    cx.region(|scope| async {
        let sem = Semaphore::new(max);
        for task in tasks {
            let permit = sem.acquire(cx).await?;
            scope.spawn(move |cx| async move {
                let _p = permit;
                task(cx).await
            });
        }
    }).await
}
```

## Unit tests
- Test: scope.spawn() runs task to completion
- Test: scope exit waits for all children
- Test: bounded spawn respects concurrency limit
- Test: cancellation propagates to all scope children
- Test: nested regions work correctly
- All tests use LabRuntime with seed-locked scheduling

## Acceptance criteria
- Structured concurrency patterns documented and tested
- Helper utilities for bounded spawn, spawn-with-timeout
- Fire-and-forget redesign strategy decided
- 5+ LabRuntime tests for the patterns

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Structured concurrency testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to exhaustively test structured concurrency guarantees:
  - Verify scope exit always waits for ALL children under every possible interleaving
  - Verify cancellation propagates to all scope children regardless of scheduling order
  - Verify nested region teardown ordering (inner scopes complete before outer)
  - Verify bounded spawn never exceeds concurrency limit under any schedule

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for task spawn overhead**: Add `benches/task_spawn.rs` measuring:
  - scope.spawn() latency for a no-op task
  - Region creation + N spawns + join overhead for N=1,10,100,1000
  - Comparison with tokio::spawn for equivalent workloads
  - Structured concurrency overhead: region vs flat spawn

## Cross-references
- See wa-brc7d.5 (mux crate in asupersync FrankenTerm crates epic) — the mux crate is the heaviest user of task spawning for pane output subscriptions and background polling. Patterns established here directly apply there.","notes":"Claimed after closing wa-1u9qw. Starting structured-concurrency migration pattern analysis and identifying first non-conflicting implementation slice.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletPond","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:49:51.413097Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:06:22.182084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1bznu","depends_on_id":"wa-3d14m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:58.430997Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"wa-1bznu","author":"CoralMeadow","text":"Progress update (CoralMeadow): landed a concrete structured-concurrency migration precursor in  via  — removed direct  import in favor of  and reduced direct  usage for two runtime loops by moving to -driven waits. This advances blocker  while preserving behavior under current runtime mode.","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:20:21Z"},{"id":205,"issue_id":"wa-1bznu","author":"CoralMeadow","text":"Correction to previous note: wa-1bznu progress landed via wa-1m7nk in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs. flow.","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:20:39Z"},{"id":208,"issue_id":"wa-1bznu","author":"CoralMeadow","text":"Additional wa-1bznu progress via wa-1m7nk: native-event coalescer wait loop in runtime.rs now uses runtime_compat::timeout pattern rather than direct tokio::select, reducing tokio-specific control-flow surface in observation runtime.","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:22:50Z"},{"id":247,"issue_id":"wa-1bznu","author":"VioletPond","text":"VioletPond progress: added structured-concurrency helper slice in crates/frankenterm-core/src/cx.rs with new APIs spawn_bounded_with_cx(...) and spawn_with_timeout(...), plus infrastructure tests in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/asupersync_cx_infrastructure.rs (bounded concurrency cap + timeout success/expiry).\n\nValidation:\n- cargo fmt --check ✅\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-violetpond-wa-1bznu cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test asupersync_cx_infrastructure ✅ (0 tests due cfg gate)\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-violetpond-wa-1bznu cargo check --all-targets ❌ pre-existing error in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_viewport_reflow_planner.rs:453 (move out of borrowed String fields)\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-violetpond-wa-1bznu cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings ❌ same pre-existing test compile error\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-violetpond-wa-1bznu cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features asupersync-runtime --test asupersync_cx_infrastructure ❌ pre-existing asupersync migration compile breakage across runtime/storage/ipc (recv/send/watch API mismatch)\n\nNext step after branch hygiene decision: wire these helpers into first module migration consumer and extend tests once asupersync feature build is green again.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:08:45Z"},{"id":266,"issue_id":"wa-1bznu","author":"SwiftOtter","text":"Progress slice (SwiftOtter, 2026-02-14): fixed asupersync watch/mpsc API mismatches across reserved runtime-adjacent modules and validated blocker movement.

Code updates:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs
  - Updated shutdown branch in server accept loop to use runtime-conditional recv future (asupersync recv(&Cx) vs tokio recv()).
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/replay.rs
  - Updated pause flow to call watch::Receiver::changed with runtime-specific signature.
  - Updated check_control() for runtime-specific watch semantics:
    - asupersync: has_changed() -> bool + borrow_and_clone()
    - tokio: has_changed().unwrap_or(false) + borrow_and_update()
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs
  - Updated periodic and intelligent scheduling loops to use runtime-conditional futures for watch changed + mpsc recv, avoiding select! branch cfg parsing issues.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs
  - Switched hot-reload sender storage to Arc<watch::Sender<HotReloadableConfig>> so runtime handle construction no longer depends on Sender::clone() availability under asupersync.

Validation:
- rustfmt --edition 2024 on touched files ✅
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftotter-wa-1bznu cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features asupersync-runtime ❌ (now fails in storage.rs only)

Resulting compiler delta:
- Cleared earlier compile blockers in ipc.rs/replay.rs/snapshot_engine.rs/runtime.rs.
- Remaining failures are concentrated in storage.rs write channel sends requiring asupersync signature migration (.send(&Cx, ...)).

Next suggested slice:
- storage.rs compat pass: convert WriteCommand sender callsites to runtime-conditional send compatibility or thread explicit Cx through StorageHandle write APIs.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:55:59Z"},{"id":268,"issue_id":"wa-1bznu","author":"AzureBay","text":"AzureBay taking non-overlap wa-1bznu slice: runtime.rs Send-bound compile fix under asupersync-runtime. Reproduced current failures with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-azurebay-wa1bznu cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features asupersync-runtime; errors are runtime.rs:1066 (future not Send via guard across await) and tailer.rs E0597. Coordinating with LilacHollow for snapshot_engine.rs/tailer.rs (they hold active reservations there).","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:06:22Z"}]}

Reproduced current failures with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-azurebay-wa1bznu cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features asupersync-runtime; errors are runtime.rs:1066 (future not Send via guard across await) and tailer.rs E0597. Coordinating with LilacHollow for snapshot_engine.rs/tailer.rs (they hold active reservations there).","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:06:22Z"},{"id":269,"issue_id":"wa-1bznu","author":"SwiftOtter","text":"Follow-up progress (SwiftOtter, 2026-02-14): expanded asupersync compatibility slice beyond initial runtime-adjacent fixes.

Additional landed compatibility updates:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs
  - Introduced WriteCommandSender wrapper to normalize write-queue sender semantics across runtimes.
  - asupersync path now calls sender.send(&Cx, cmd); tokio path keeps sender.send(cmd).
  - Added runtime-specific max_capacity fallback (asupersync uses channel capacity).
  - Updated writer_loop recv path to support asupersync receiver via runtime block_on(rx.recv(&Cx).await.ok()) while preserving tokio blocking_recv path.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs
  - Added runtime-compat helpers for watch and mpsc behavior:
    - watch_has_changed
    - watch_borrow_and_update_clone
    - mpsc_max_capacity
    - mpsc_recv_option
  - Migrated config hot-reload callsites and capture relay recv path to these helpers.
  - Refactored discovery new-pane handling to avoid holding registry write guard across async awaits.
  - Refactored detection-context handling to avoid holding context write guard across async engine lock awaits.
  - Kept config sender as Arc<watch::Sender<_>> to avoid asupersync watch Sender clone limitation.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/tailer.rs
  - Fixed asupersync reserve lifetime by keeping reserve Cx in scope while SendPermit is live.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs
  - ensure_session no longer holds session_id read guard across await (prevents non-Send future in runtime spawn path).

Validation:
- rustfmt on touched files ✅
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftotter-wa-1bznu-v2 cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features asupersync-runtime ✅
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftotter-wa-1bznu-v2 cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets --features asupersync-runtime ❌

Current remaining blockers are now primarily test-path adapters under asupersync runtime (not core lib path):
- ipc.rs test shutdown channel send(()).await callsites need send(&Cx,...)
- replay.rs tests rely on watch::Sender::clone()
- runtime.rs/storage.rs tests rely on mpsc Sender::max_capacity(), send(value), and Receiver::recv() tokio signatures
- snapshot_engine.rs tests use rx.recv().await tokio signature

Net effect: core feature path for frankenterm-core now typechecks under asupersync; all-targets test suite still needs dedicated compat pass.","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:11:36Z"},{"id":271,"issue_id":"wa-1bznu","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"LilacHollow compile-unblock slice complete: snapshot_engine mark_shutdown now clones session_id before awaiting spawn_blocking DB call, removing non-Send RwLockReadGuard capture that was breaking runtime tokio::spawn path under asupersync feature builds. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-lilachollow-wa-1bznu cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features asupersync-runtime => PASS. Focused behavior check: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-lilachollow-wa-1bznu cargo test -p frankenterm-core mark_shutdown_sets_flag -- --nocapture => target test passed. Workspace cargo check --all-targets passes; clippy/fmt still fail on unrelated pre-existing files (e.g., dead_code in proptest_output_table + existing rustfmt drift in multiple proptest files).","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:18:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-1c2u","title":"Smart Scrollback with Importance Weighting","description":"## Goal
Replace WezTerm's fixed scrollback limit with importance-weighted scrollback that retains high-value output (errors, tool results, compilation output) longer than low-value output (blank lines, repeated prompts), reducing memory usage while preserving the content that matters.

## Background & Motivation
WezTerm's scrollback is a fixed-size ring buffer (default 3,500 lines per pane). With 50 panes, that's 175,000 lines of terminal cell data in memory. But scrollback content varies enormously in value:
- High value: error messages, compilation output, test results, tool_use boundaries
- Low value: blank lines, repeated prompts, ANSI cursor movements, progress bars

Fixed limits waste memory on low-value content while potentially evicting high-value content.

## Technical Design

### Importance Scoring
```rust
pub struct ScrollbackLine {
    pub cells: Vec<Cell>,
    pub importance: f64,  // 0.0 (garbage) to 1.0 (critical)
    pub timestamp: Instant,
}

fn score_line(line: &str, context: &PaneContext) -> f64 {
    let mut score = 0.3;  // baseline

    // High value indicators
    if patterns::is_error(line) { score += 0.4; }
    if patterns::is_tool_boundary(line) { score += 0.3; }
    if patterns::is_compilation_output(line) { score += 0.2; }
    if patterns::is_test_result(line) { score += 0.3; }

    // Low value indicators
    if line.trim().is_empty() { score -= 0.2; }
    if patterns::is_progress_bar(line) { score -= 0.3; }
    if patterns::is_ansi_only(line) { score -= 0.3; }

    score.clamp(0.0, 1.0)
}
```

### Eviction Policy
Instead of FIFO eviction, use importance-weighted eviction:
```rust
fn evict_line(scrollback: &mut VecDeque<ScrollbackLine>) -> Option<ScrollbackLine> {
    // Find lowest importance line in oldest 25% of scrollback
    let quarter = scrollback.len() / 4;
    let (min_idx, _) = scrollback.iter()
        .take(quarter)
        .enumerate()
        .min_by(|(_, a), (_, b)| a.importance.partial_cmp(&b.importance).unwrap())
        .unwrap();
    scrollback.remove(min_idx)
}
```

### Memory Budget
Instead of line count, use byte budget per pane:
```rust
pub struct ScrollbackConfig {
    pub byte_budget_per_pane: usize,  // default: 2MB
    pub min_lines: usize,             // always keep at least this many (500)
    pub max_lines: usize,             // hard cap (10,000)
    pub importance_threshold: f64,    // never evict above this (0.8)
}
```

### Integration with patterns.rs
Reuses existing pattern detection from patterns.rs (3-stage Bloom→Aho-Corasick→Regex) for line classification. The importance scorer is a thin wrapper over existing pattern rules.

### Implementation Location
- Modify: FrankenTerm fork's terminal/src/terminalstate/mod.rs (scrollback management)
- Integration: patterns.rs provides line classification
- Config: scrollback section in wa-core config

## Existing Code References
- patterns.rs: 100+ pattern rules, 3-stage matching (reuse for line classification)
- FrankenTerm terminal crate: scrollback ring buffer (modify eviction policy)

## Configuration
```toml
[scrollback]
mode = "importance_weighted"  # "fixed" | "importance_weighted"
byte_budget_per_pane_mb = 2
min_lines = 500
max_lines = 10000
importance_threshold = 0.8    # Never evict lines above this
```

## Dependencies
- FrankenTerm fork (bd-20fw): modifying terminal scrollback internals
- patterns.rs: reuse pattern rules for importance scoring
- Enhances memory reduction (wa-3kxe): less scrollback memory per pane

## Acceptance Criteria
- Importance scoring for each scrollback line
- Weighted eviction: low-value lines evicted before high-value
- Byte budget instead of line count
- Integration with existing pattern rules
- Configurable thresholds and budgets
- Unit tests: synthetic scrollback, verify high-value retention
- Memory benchmark: RSS reduction vs fixed scrollback under 50-pane load

## Benchmark Requirements
- **criterion benchmarks** with target `benches/importance_scoring.rs`:
  - `score_line_latency`: measure time to compute importance score for a single line. Target: <1us per line (must be fast enough for inline scoring during terminal output).
  - `score_batch_throughput`: measure scoring throughput for 10,000 lines of mixed content. Target: >1M lines/sec.
  - `eviction_selection`: measure time to find the lowest-importance line in the oldest 25% of a 10,000-line scrollback. Target: <50us per eviction decision.
  - `byte_budget_enforcement`: measure overhead of byte-budget tracking vs simple line-count tracking. Target: <5% overhead.

## Property-Based Testing (proptest)
- **Scoring monotonicity**: if line A matches strictly more high-value indicators than line B (and no more low-value indicators), then `score(A) >= score(B)`. More signals of importance always result in equal or higher scores.
- **Score range invariant**: for any input string, `score_line()` returns a value in [0.0, 1.0]. No combination of pattern matches can produce a score outside this range.
- **Eviction ordering**: lines with importance >= `importance_threshold` are never evicted while lines with importance < `importance_threshold` exist in the scrollback. The threshold is a hard floor.
- **Budget compliance**: after any sequence of insertions and evictions, `total_bytes <= byte_budget_per_pane` (within one line's tolerance for the last insertion that triggered eviction).

## Cross-References
- **wa-3r5e** (Scrollback memory pressure mitigation): wa-3r5e makes pane-level decisions about how aggressively to trim scrollback under memory pressure, while wa-1c2u makes line-level decisions about which lines to keep. Under pressure, wa-3r5e reduces the byte budget, and wa-1c2u uses importance scoring to decide which lines survive within that reduced budget.
- **wa-283h4.8** (Entropy-aware scheduling): entropy measurements can inform importance scoring — lines with high information entropy (novel content) should score higher than low-entropy lines (repeated patterns). This provides a principled, information-theoretic foundation for the heuristic scoring rules.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkDune","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:14.755885Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:30.480875Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1c2u","depends_on_id":"wa-20fw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:16:56.597823Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-1c2u","depends_on_id":"wa-3kxe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:32.328834Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-1c2u","depends_on_id":"wa-ixt4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:16:56.597823Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":94,"issue_id":"wa-1c2u","author":"PinkDune","text":"Implemented no-conflict wa-1c2u slice in existing scrollback files: added line-importance scoring (PatternEngine-integrated), weighted oldest-window eviction with threshold floor, byte-budget enforcement helpers, additive unit tests, new proptests for score-range/monotonicity/threshold-floor/budget-compliance, and criterion benchmarks for scoring+selection+budget overhead in benches/scrollback_eviction.rs. Validation blocked by pre-existing unrelated parse errors in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs (mismatched delimiters around lines ~830/1157), so full cargo check/test cannot currently complete.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:30Z"}]}

## Core requirement
Ship this safely with transparent compatibility guarantees and robust operational controls.

## Required artifacts
- no-regression compatibility contract
- staged canary rollout with rollback criteria
- observability dashboards + alerts
- incident response and rollback runbooks
- user-facing tuning/troubleshooting guidance
- formal go/no-go evidence bundle

## Release philosophy
No subjective confidence-only release; objective gates and evidence decide readiness.

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","title":"Define no-regression compatibility contract for terminal semantics and user workflows","description":"## Context
Resize improvements are only successful if terminal semantics and common workflows remain correct.

## Scope
Define the no-regression compatibility contract as release policy, covering cursor behavior, wrapping semantics, scrollback integrity, alt-screen behavior, and interaction continuity.

## Deliverables
- Compatibility contract document with explicit invariant list
- Mapping from invariants to validation suites and artifact evidence
- Escalation process when contract checks fail

## Why this matters
This protects users from subtle behavioral regressions masked by performance gains.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contract enumerates all critical user-visible semantics and invariants.
- Each invariant is mapped to at least one automated test/e2e assertion.
- Alt-screen and interaction-continuity checks are explicitly included.
- Contract is referenced by rollout and final go/no-go decision beads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:48.126624Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:53:49.899497Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compatibility","reflow","resize","track-rollout","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:37.236341Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:37.340057Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:37.443933Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:48.126624Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","title":"Execute final go/no-go review and archive evidence bundle for sign-off","description":"## Context
Final release promotion requires a single authoritative evidence package and decision record.

## Scope
Execute formal go/no-go review using objective evidence from correctness, performance, reliability, and user-perception tracks.

## Deliverables
- Consolidated evidence bundle (unit/integration/e2e/perf/soak outputs)
- Risk register with residual risks and mitigation ownership
- Signed decision record with explicit release rationale and rollback readiness

## Why this matters
Ensures release quality and accountability are evidence-driven, not impression-driven.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence bundle includes pass/fail summaries and links to detailed artifacts for all required tracks.
- No unresolved critical blocker remains; exceptions are explicitly approved and documented.
- Rollback plan, kill-switch verification, and on-call readiness are confirmed.
- Decision record references beta feedback and SLO compliance data.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:48.654838Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:53:50.003001Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["governance","reflow","release","resize","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:39.027356Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:40.481825Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.7.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:40.581750Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.7.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:40.681790Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:48.654838Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:38.503832Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:38.608403Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:38.712150Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:38.817612Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:38.923234Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:40.782319Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
